programme.desc: It's a two-story mobile mansion for Simon Cowell, a tough as nails two-bedroom rig from Showhauler and a NASA inspired trailer of the future - the Cricket.

programme.title: Getaway Driver
programme.desc: Two drifters arrive at the compound with a BMW E36 and a modded-out Mustang. The more experienced third driver has come to teach them a lesson.

programme.title: Getaway Driver
programme.desc: Heavy rain turns the compound into a wasteland. A '55 Dodge pickup, Nissan 240SX and a '94 Ford Taurus scour it for traction and an exit.

programme.title: Street Outlaws: No Prep Kings
programme.desc: The pressure is on and despite being so late in the season, some racers still have car problems. Team Kye and Team Dave fight to stay ahead.

programme.title: Street Outlaws: The Great Eight
programme.desc: With the season nearing its end, everyone is trying to catch up to Kye, the points leader. Axman, in second, sees a chance to move ahead.

programme.title: Food Truck Nation
programme.desc: Brad visits a food truck with a custom smoker, tries classic Cajun dishes with a twist and in LA, discovers an ice cream truck without a freezer.

programme.title: Food Truck Nation
programme.desc: Innovative spins on the classic grilled cheese sandwich; a veritable sushi bar on wheels with fresh tuna and wagyu beef and fresh funnel cake.

programme.title: Truck Dynasty
programme.desc: Jerry Jr. offers free customisation to chef Guy Fieri for an Apocalypse Hellfire truck he bought at auction. Frank locks in a custom deal.

programme.title: FourWheeler
programme.desc: Ian builds a 2-door Jeep Gladiator with an LS V-8 engine. He takes the old V-6 and shows the things people forget when they do a V-8 swap.

programme.title: FourWheeler
programme.desc: Ian works on a custom All-Aluminum Land Rover Defender that sits on top of a Jeep JK chassis. The Cummins R2.8 engine will be the cherry on top.

programme.title: Aussie Truck Rehab
programme.desc: A lifelong dream truck threatens to turn into a nightmare. Owner Jon Kelly's decision to repaint two near new trucks risks a $700,000 sale.

programme.title: Kindig Customs
programme.desc: Dave tries to come up with a one-of-a-kind colour that incorporates the client request to get purple into the build.

programme.title: Car Fix
programme.desc: Jeremy Bumpus and Bryan Fuller bring in a classic Thunderbird. They show how to up this classic car's performance and fix a paint job.

programme.title: Car Fix
programme.desc: Why is hydroboost so crucial in your brakes? The guys will show you how to convert hydroboost under the floor on a vintage Bel Air.

programme.title: Fastest Cars In The Dirty S
programme.desc: Eric Malone has been called in to rescue an 06' Mustang that was top of the line in its heyday but has been sitting in a garage for a decade.

programme.title: Dirty Mudder Truckers
programme.desc: Team South Carolina head down to Florida to challenge The Dirty 7. They will have to push their motors to the limit and battle it out in The Pit.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie has to have his tonsils removed and will miss a big Halloween party.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Richie, Potsie, and Ralph get an apartment of their own.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Pinky's sister, Leather, comes to Milwaukee looking for a gig. Al gives her and her group a job at Arnold's after a successful audition, but Officer Kirk threatens to shut the place down.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: After Howard refuses to let Joanie go on tour with Leather, she follows through with her threat to run away from home.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie is invited to a socialite dinner party, but the people who invite him do so thinking Fonzie will make a fool of himself.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Richie, Potsie, and Ralph want to join the best fraternity on campus, but they have to survive 'Hell Week' first.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: After getting a D on a paper for his investigative reporting class, Richie decides to do a story about the corrupt sanitation department.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Howard's father comes for a visit after being forcibly retired as a captain of detectives due to his age. Not wanting to sit around, he gets a job at Fonzie's garage.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Potsie decides he wants to marry Jennifer Jerome, his girlfriend, after one date. Howard instead suggests that Potsie get pinned instead, but Potsie backs out after asking her.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Joanie is set to go on her first car date, so Fonzie and Richie show her how to counter any advances her date may make toward her. However, Joanie would like a chance for her first kiss.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: When Marion's friend is dumped by her husband for a younger woman, Marion begins to think that Howard may do the same to her.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Lori Beth reports that Richie isn't moving after they crash his new motorcycle.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie's dog, Spunky, appears depressed and distant. After a visit to the veterinarian, it is determined that Spunky's problem is mental.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Joanie daydreams about the gang singing romantic Valentine's Day songs.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Richie tells Chachi the story of how he met Fonzie in order to convince him that joining a gang is a mistake.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: An alien, Mork from Ork, comes to Earth looking for someone average or humdrum to study, and ends up picking Richie.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Lori Beth has to write a term paper on the typical, middle-class family, so she interviews each Cunningham family member as well as their friends.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie's girlfriend is a moderately successful ballet teacher, but Fonzie has trouble accepting that she may be happier as a professional performer in New York.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Al is to receive a man-of-the-year award from the Sons of Italy, but he doesn't have a date. Fonzie sets him up with a meter maid, and she agrees to go with him to the banquet.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Richie and Lori Beth have a fight and break up before going on a weekend trip where the band is backing up Leather and the Suedes. Meanwhile, Howard and Marion argue about women's rights.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie and Howard are picked for jury duty. The case appears cut and dry, but Fonzie isn't so sure. Meanwhile, Richie, Potsie and Ralph practice for a fraternity festival.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: The entire gang goes to Colorado to help Marion's Uncle Ben at his dude ranch. They have to come up with enough money in five days to pay off Ben's neighbour who holds a note on the ranch.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: The gang stages a rodeo in an attempt to raise money to pay off the note HR Buchanan holds on Marion's Uncle Ben's ranch.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Richie is able to stop the out-of-control horses that are pulling a wagon Joanie is in. With the deadline looming, the gang is still short, so Fonzie volunteers to ride a killer bull for a $1000 prize.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Al accidentally hits Fonzie in the head with a serving tray, and he becomes blind as a result.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Richie makes up a story to tell Lori Beth when he's actually escorting a baton twirler to the fraternity dance.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie and his girlfriend's son are getting along together as if they were father and son, but his girlfriend reveals that she and her estranged husband are getting back together.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Joanie thinks she's found true love in Jefferson High's star quarterback. However, she sees him sweet-talking another girl, breaks up with him, and is faced with not having a date for her sweet 16 party.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Richie is writing a story about fears and phobias, and he finds out that Ralph is afraid of nearly everything.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Al fears that an elderly lady has placed a curse on him.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Al thinks about taking on an investment partner to create an Arnold's franchise, but when Al suggests consulting an accountant and lawyer beforehand, the man he's dealing with calls in his goons.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.sub-title: The Fonz Is Allergic To Girls
programme.desc: Fonzie comes to think that he is allergic to girls.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Upset that the family is watching a football game instead of helping her prepare the Thanksgiving dinner, Marion reminds them of the meaning of the holiday with the story of the first Thanksgiving.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Richie's reputation goes from respectable to racy after he's identified in a police line-up as the notorious Kissing Bandit.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Howard and Al are co-chairmen of a Leopard Lodge fundraiser, but the magician/escape artist they hire injures himself while attempting the milk-can escape.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Richie, who is running for class president, gets his picture taken at a massage parlour by his opponent. Richie thinks his campaign will be ruined if the picture gets out.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie receives a Christmas gift from his father but is reluctant to accept and open it. Meanwhile, Joanie is upset at Howard for getting an artificial tree.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Joanie starts smoking to fit in with a club she's recently joined. Howard initially forbids her to smoke but, eventually, allows her to make her own decision.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Richie temporarily takes over a local newspaper's advice column but only tells Fonzie about it. Potsie and Ralph, in an attempt to solve their continuous bickering, write in to Richie's column.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Leather and the band audition for a television music show and are turned down. Leather's song, however, is stolen and recorded by another band.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: It's Howard and Marion's 23rd anniversary. Richie, Fonzie and Joanie have arranged for them to take a second honeymoon, but Marion wants everything to be exactly the same as it was on their first honeymoon.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Ralph asks Leather to the annual military ball. Fonzie teaches Ralph to act like a serious gentleman, and Marion teaches Leather to dress and act like an elegant lady.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie discovers thousands of dollars in counterfeit money hidden in a casket inside of a hearse he is working on. When he doesn't return after returning the hearse, the guys fear the worst.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie survives an explosion at the garage triggered by the counterfeiters. The counterfeiters don't know that Fonzie has survived, so a phony funeral is staged at their funeral parlour to entrap them.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Mork returns to 1950s Milwaukee to learn about relationships in this retrospective episode.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: A fencing champion from France visits UWM for an exhibition. After he insults Joanie and America, Richie challenges the champion to a duel.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Needing extra money, Chachi begins selling Incredo-Wax, a miracle product that will shine anything. Unfortunately, it has disastrous side effects and ruins everything it touches.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Potsie threatens to quit school and Joanie and Howard are at odds over her less than stellar report card.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: The entire gang goes to Colorado to help Marion's Uncle Ben at his dude ranch. They have to come up with enough money in five days to pay off Ben's neighbour who holds a note on the ranch.

programme.title: Fifi and the Flowertots

programme.title: Fifi and the Flowertots

programme.title: Madagascar: A Little Wild

programme.title: AFL: R13 GEE v GC
programme.desc: Two of the hottest teams clash in Geelong where the Suns will be looking for their first win at the venue.

programme.title: AFL: R13 GWS v PA
programme.desc: The Giants are coming off two wins and will host Port Adelaide after a bye and looking to break a string of losses.

programme.title: AFL: R13 NM v WC
programme.desc: Both teams see this as one to win; the Roos fresh off the bye and the Eagles riding high from impressing the competition last week.

programme.title: AFLW: Grand Final NMT v BRI
programme.desc: Competition powerhouses North Melbourne and Brisbane clash in the AFLW Grand Final for the third year in a row at Ikon Park.

programme.title: Best Games Ever: 1993 PF ESS v ADE
programme.desc: 1993 Preliminary Final. The Crows had the Baby Bombers on the ropes to half time, before Kevin Sheedy's men click into gear in the second half.

programme.title: Best Games Ever: 2013 R13 BRI v GEE
programme.desc: 2013 Round 13. The Miracle on Grass. The Cats were cruising at the Gabba, but the Lions never, ever gave up - coming from 7 goals down to having a chance to win.

programme.title: Best Games Ever: 2001 R16 NM v ESS
programme.desc: 2001 Round 16. The Kangaroos were 10 goals up, but the Bombers rolled up their sleeves and got to work, closing the gap all the way to the final siren.

programme.title: Best Games Ever: 2002 GF BRI v COL
programme.desc: 2002 Grand Final. The Lions were looking at back-to-back flags. The Pies for their first since 1990. This brutal Grand Final would go down as one of the best of the modern era.

programme.title: Best Games Ever: 2005 SF SYD v GEE
programme.desc: 2005 Semi Final. Geelong head to the SCG to face the Swans in a scrappy, tense affair before a one-man last-quarter clinic blindsides us all.

programme.title: Best Games Ever: 2017 EF PA v WCE
programme.desc: 2017 Elimination Final. When the siren sounded, the Eagles and Power were tied. When the extra time siren sounded, they were tied. At the end of DOUBLE OVERTIME, they were still tied. It all hinges on a shot after the third final siren.

programme.title: Best Games Ever: 2008 GF HAW v GEE
programme.desc: 2008 Grand Final. The Cats at the peak of the powers against a Hawks side led by mastermind coach Alastair Clarkson, who called the Cats a 'shark' that couldn't breathe if it wasn't moving. A coaching move that started his legacy.

programme.title: Best Games Ever: 2009 R5 ESS v COL
programme.desc: 2009 Round 5. Anzac Day between the Bombers and the Pies. A wet afternoon at the G, it was a slog all day until David Zaharakis announced himself with seconds left in the game.

programme.title: Best Games Ever: 2009 GF GEE v STK
programme.desc: 2009 Grand Final. The Saints and the Cats were far and away the best teams of '09. And thy put on one of the best Grand Finals - a brutal tussle that was only broken by moments of sheer brilliance.

programme.title: Best Games Ever: 2012 GF HAW v SYD
programme.desc: 2012 Grand Final. This Grand Final had it all. Massive hits, blistering runs and moments that have lived forever in footy folklore on a perfect day in September.

programme.title: Best Games Ever: 2009 R14 STK v GEE
programme.desc: 2009 Round 14. The Cats and the Saints put on what is considered the best home-and-away game ever played. Blistering and brutal from start to finish it's two powerhouse teams at their peak going at it from siren to siren.

programme.title: Best Games Ever: 2013 PF GEE v HAW
programme.desc: 2013 Preliminary Final. The Hawks and the Cats - all they do is play in blockbusters. This one is no different as they fight tooth and nail for a spot in the 2013 Grand Final.

programme.title: Best Games Ever: 1999 PF ESS v CAR
programme.desc: 1999 Preliminary Final. The Bombers and the Blues duke it out on the MCG for the right to play in a Grand Final. A match that has gone down in footy folklore thanks to that tackle.

programme.title: Best Games Ever: 2006 GF SYD v WCE
programme.desc: 2006 Grand Final. Sydney and West Coast put on a Grand Final for the ages in 2005, and the very next year they did it again - going toe-to-toe until the bitter end.

programme.title: Best Games Ever: 2010 GF COL v STK
programme.desc: 2010 Grand Final. The Pies and the Saints meet on footy's biggest stage and put on a game that no-one will ever forget, where the bounce of the ball changed footy forever.

programme.title: Best Games Ever: 2005 GF SYD v WCE
programme.desc: 2005 Grand Final. Sydney and West Coast play off as the Swans aim to break the longest premiership drought in the AFL - 72 years.

programme.title: Best Games Ever: 2016 GF WB v SYD
programme.desc: 2016 Grand Final. The Bulldogs came from 7th to make a Grand Final in 2016, taking the hard road to face the Swans. Without their captain they must find one more big effort to break their premiership drought.

programme.title: Best Games Ever: 1989 GF GEE v HAW
programme.desc: 1989 Grand Final. Considered one of the best games ever played, this is a Grand Final everyone needs to watch at least once. Hits, hangers and highlights from start to finish in an old-school slugfest for a Premiership.

programme.title: Best Games Ever: 2016 PF GWS v WB
programme.desc: 2016 Preliminary Final. The Giants and the Dogs have a short, but fierce rivalry. Player defections, dirty tactics and high-stakes matches fuelled the fire for this one which didn't disappoint.

programme.title: Best Games Ever: 2018 GF COL v WCE
programme.desc: 2018 Grand Final. West Coast v Collingwood put on a frenetic Grand Final that hung in the balance until the dying seconds. History hinged on the smallest moments all day in a match that might be the best we've seen in the modern era.

programme.title: Best Games Ever: 1994 PF GEE V NM
programme.desc: 1994 Preliminary Final. The Roos and the Cats face off for a spot in the 1994 Grand Final. The game rests on Mick Martyn v Gary Ablett Senior in a match that goes right down to the wire.

programme.title: Best Games Ever: 1994 R7 SYD v STK
programme.desc: 1994 Round 7. The Swans and the Saints go at it in a see-sawing clash way back in 1994. The game's greatest goalkicker - Tony Lockett - has a game for the ages as he tries to drag his Saints over the line.

programme.title: Best Games Ever: 1996 PF SYD v ESS
programme.desc: 1996 Preliminary Final. A Grand Final spot is on the line when the Bombers head to the SCG to take on the Swans. A brutal game that goes right down to the wire.

programme.title: Best Games Ever: 1986 EF FIT v ESS
programme.desc: 1986 Elimination Final. Fitzroy's last finals series before merging with Brisbane, the Roy Boys put on a farewell show for the ages against Bombers in front of 60,000 fans.

programme.title: Best Games Ever: 2004 R11 ESS v HAW
programme.desc: 2004, Round 11. Essendon and the Hawks have hated each other for generations. But the now infamous 'Line in the Sand' game would stoke the fire for years to come. This is unrelenting, unapologetic, unsociable footy.

programme.title: Best Games Ever: 1977 GF NM v COL
programme.desc: 1977 Grand Final. The Roos and the Pies play out one of the most memorable Grand Finals in history.

programme.title: Best Games Ever: 1998 R22 NM v WB
programme.desc: 1998, Round 22. The 'King' Wayne Carey is arguably the best to have ever played - and he shows us why in this game against the Dogs, in a last quarter that has to be seen to be believed.

programme.title: Best Games Ever: 2013 R19 PA v ADE
programme.desc: 2013, Round 19. The Showdowns between Adelaide and Port Adelaide are essential viewing every year, but this one is right up there with the best, including a goal - and a finish - that lived on long after the final siren.

programme.title: Best Games Ever: 1979 GF CAR v COL
programme.desc: 1979 Grand Final. Any time you get the Pies and the Blues in a big game you can bet there will be fireworks, but throw them both into a Grand Final and you get something else entirely.

programme.title: Best Games Ever: 2000 R21 FRE v WCE
programme.desc: 2000 Round 21. The WA Derby looks a fizzer when the Eagles go 42 points up, until Dale Kickett fires up and the Dockers start to close the gap, and fast.

programme.title: AFL: R13 CAR v ESS
programme.desc: The Blues host the Bombers in a massive Sunday night clash at the MCG.

programme.title: High-Rise
programme.desc: A doctor moves into a London skyscraper where rising tensions and class warfare lead to anarchy.

programme.title: Cheerful Weather for the Wedding
programme.desc: Dolly is in turmoil when her old lover appears on her wedding day. Does she run away with him or choose a humdrum husband? Astute comedy about family foibles.

programme.title: The Seagull
programme.desc: A fading actress, a fresh-faced ingenue, a famous writer and a failing playwright all come up against one another while visiting a country estate.

programme.title: The Bookshop
programme.desc: Set in a small town in 1959 England, a woman decides, against polite but ruthless local opposition, to open a bookshop, a decision which becomes a political minefield.

programme.title: Mary Shelley
programme.desc: The love affair between poet Percy Shelley and 18-year-old Mary Wollstonecraft Godwin, which resulted in Mary Shelley writing Frankenstein.

programme.title: Alone in Berlin
programme.desc: In the heart of terror in 1940, two German people resist everyday life and have an extraordinary impact.

programme.title: Churchill
programme.desc: A ticking-clock thriller following Winston Churchill in the 96 hours before D-day.

programme.title: Death at a Funeral
programme.desc: A dignified send-off for a loved one erupts into uproarious chaos when a myriad of outrageous calamities befall the eccentric grief stricken mourners.

programme.title: McClaren
programme.desc: The story of Bruce McLaren, the New Zealander who showed the world that a man of humble beginnings could take on the elite of motor racing and win.

programme.title: Comes a Bright Day
programme.desc: Love blooms in the most unusual circumstances when a bellboy and a beautiful shopgirl are taken hostage in a jewel store heist.

programme.title: Cheri
programme.desc: In this superb drama set in pre-World War I Paris, an ageing courtesan has an ill-fated love affair with the son of her rival.

programme.title: The Secret Scripture
programme.desc: A woman committed to a mental hospital for much of her life must rely on her diary to recount her memories to a doctor who is reassessing her for the move to a new facility.

programme.title: Wild Target
programme.desc: An assassin with a peerless reputation for lethal efficiency finds himself drawn to one of his intended victims.

programme.title: Mr. Turner
programme.desc: An acclaimed account of the great British painter who was a radical and revolutionary artist.

programme.title: Felicia's Journey
programme.desc: A lovesick young woman searches for the father of her unborn child in seedy Birmingham and becomes involved with a mysterious catering manager.

programme.title: Josh Jarman
programme.desc: Marcus Graham plays Josh Jarman, a struggling playwright who has written a long, serious play about doomed love, failed relationships and the overall hurt and heartache of falling in love.

programme.title: Head On
programme.desc: A young Greek-Australian man who struggles with his sexuality and the traditional expectations of his parents.

programme.title: Attack Force Z
programme.desc: Australian commandos rescue survivors of a downed aircraft on a Japanese-held island.

programme.title: Hounds of Love
programme.desc: In the summer of 1987, 17-year-old Vicky Maloney is abducted by a perverse couple, John and Evelyn White.

programme.title: Number 96
programme.desc: The Number 96 crew return with romance, mishaps, secrets, and surprises. Vera finds love and danger, Dorrie's anniversary is rocked, and Sonia fears she's losing her mind.

programme.title: Round the Twist
programme.desc: Pete uses magic lipstick to become irresistible.

programme.title: Round the Twist

programme.desc: Richie is writing a story about fears and phobias, and he finds out that Ralph is afraid of nearly everything.

programme.desc: Al thinks about taking on an investment partner to create an Arnold's franchise, but when Al suggests consulting an accountant and lawyer beforehand, the man he's dealing with calls in his goons.

programme.desc: Richie temporarily takes over a local newspaper's advice column but only tells Fonzie about it. Potsie and Ralph, in an attempt to solve their continuous bickering, write in to Richie's column.

programme.title: Celebrations with Lacey Chabert
programme.desc: Teen Justin has worked tirelessly to save nearly 80 acres in the Rainforests of Ecuador, home to many species of frogs. Lacey and her team are throwing him Rainforest Celebration to honour his efforts.

programme.title: Celebrations with Lacey Chabert
programme.desc: Sophie volunteers at Ride On Therapeutic Horsemanship Center, assisting young riders and caring for the animals. Now, Lacey teams up with Lisa and Marisa to celebrate Sophie with a pre-Bat Mitzvah Party at the Barn.

programme.title: New Creation Church
programme.sub-title: Much More Restoration Is Coming! Pt 3 of 4
programme.desc: Pastor Joseph Prince preaches messages centred on the pure gospel of the grace of God. His desire is to help believers understand grace and how deeply loved they are by their heavenly Father.

programme.title: Bible Man
programme.desc: Miles Peterson had everything: Money, power, fame...Until he lost it. Then Miles finds the power of God and harnesses that power to become Bibleman.

programme.title: Sea Kids

programme.title: LIFE Church - Online
programme.desc: Join Senior Pastors Paul and Maree de Jong along with the LIFE Team for their Sunday service experience, pre-recorded from their Central Campus in Auckland, New Zealand.

programme.title: Alaska Missionaries

programme.title: Joyce Meyer: Enjoying Everyday Life
programme.sub-title: Living in Agreement with God - Part 1
programme.desc: Do you have a right attitude about your past? Today Joyce teaches what the Bible says about letting go of your past and agreeing with God and moving forward into your future.

programme.title: Joel Osteen
programme.desc: Direct from Houston, Texas, join Joel and Victoria Osteen and the team from Lakewood Church for a session of powerful music and empowering preaching.

programme.title: Songs of Praise

programme.title: Ragamuffin
programme.desc: Rich Mullins rose to fame and fortune in the music industry and then gave it all away to live in obscurity among Native Americans on a reservation in New Mexico.

programme.title: Kids Beach Club

programme.title: Ancestors: New Testament
programme.sub-title: Mary and Martha, Syrophoenician Woman, Sheep

programme.desc: It appears Demetri Chicopoulos is living a charmed life, but he has lost his mother and recently gone through a divorce. In a prayerful moment Demetri asks God if he could just relive one week of his life.

programme.desc: Family-friendly comedians present a night of clean, feel-good laughter. Featuring top talent who prove you don't need crude jokes to be funny, this series delivers quick wit.

programme.sub-title: Set Free to Follow God Pt23

programme.title: Elevation Experience
programme.desc: The Elevation Experience with Pastor Steven Furtick features inspirational messages from Pastor Steven as well as original worship music from Elevation Church in Charlotte, North Carolina.

programme.sub-title: Living in Agreement with God - Part 1
programme.desc: Do you have a right attitude about your past? Today Joyce teaches what the Bible says about letting go of your past and agreeing with God and moving forward into your future.

programme.desc: It appears Demetri Chicopoulos is living a charmed life, but he has lost his mother and recently gone through a divorce. In a prayerful moment Demetri asks God if he could just relive one week of his life.

programme.title: The Jackal
programme.desc: An FBI director works with a Russian agent and a jailed terrorist to hunt down the elusive Jackal, a master-of-disguise assassin on a deadly mission.

programme.title: Kelly's Heroes
programme.desc: A classic comedy based around a group of WWII soldiers who plan to steal a treasure trove from the Nazis.

programme.title: Groundhog Day

programme.title: The Karate Kid: Part II

programme.title: The Addams Family (1991)
programme.desc: A swindler claiming to be their long-lost Uncle Fester tries to steal a fortune from the kooky and spooky Addams family.

programme.desc: A sting at one Australia's most remote airports ends with a significant seizure. Officers are amazed to find a whole new method to smuggling drugs.

programme.title: Border Security: Australia's Front Line
programme.desc: An American's behaviour becomes increasingly bizarre and officers need to predict what his next move will be. Customs shut the door on a major drug shipment from Hong Kong.

programme.title: Border Security: Australia's Front Line
programme.desc: A passenger makes a strange admission that he is here to deliver a children's book, but officers have a different read on the situation.

programme.title: Border Security: Australia's Front Line
programme.desc: It was supposed to be a romantic holiday for a young couple, but a customs search has a very unhappy ending. A man gets into trouble and claims he had no idea of what is in his bags.

programme.title: Paramedics

programme.title: Cycle Around Japan
programme.desc: Each of the Yatsugatake highlands has a unique character. We see kids discover themselves on horseback, eat prehistoric-style and go stargazing with an astronomer.

programme.title: Rockie and Her Friends
programme.desc: Puppet action show for children. In a distant post-human future where surviving creatures have formed a community, new-breed dinosaur girl Rockie sets out on hilarious adventures with her schoolmates.

programme.title: NHK Newsline
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: J-Melo
programme.desc: Join May J. for Japanese music! This week, two singer-songwriters: ex-Vocaloid producer Kamiyama Yoh, whose fanbase extends to China; and noa, who crafts music, lyrics, arrangements, and art.

programme.title: Little Charo
programme.desc: This program is an animation series about a Japanese dog, Charo. He meets and is helped by many mythical creatures of Northern Japan as he searches for eight star talismans. Tohoku series 1-1.

programme.title: Japanology Plus Mini
programme.desc: Goldfish in Japan range from beautiful to comical. A single ranchu - known as the "king of goldfish" - can cost millions of yen. In a 5-minute digest, we explore why they are so beloved.

programme.title: A Cat's-Eye View of Japan
programme.desc: Mama cat Koyuki lives at a pottery workshop and is raising two playful kittens. Taisho, who lives at a fruit shop, keeps an eye on the shopping arcade. Both cats love resting on their owner's laps!

programme.title: NHK Newsline
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: The World Heritage Sites in Japan
programme.desc: Shirakami-Sanchi, or the Shirakami Mountain Range, is home to one of the world's largest beech forests. Its pristine environment hosts a variety of wildlife, as well as a group of hunter-gatherers.

programme.title: Okinawa: Homes
programme.desc: Okinawa Prefecture is a group of subtropical islands in the far south of Japan. We examine its past and present, with footage from time-lapse creator Shimizu Daisuke. This time: Homes.

programme.title: Grand Sumo Highlights
programme.desc: NHK WORLD-JAPAN presents a sumo highlights program for fans around the globe. Today the show features all top-division bouts from January 11, Day 1 of the Grand Sumo Tournament in Tokyo.

programme.title: Sumopedia
programme.desc: Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with information on the techniques, traditions and famous wrestlers of the past. The rules may be simple but there is a lot more to sumo than meets the eye.

programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Laugh Then Think:Japan's Offbeat Science
programme.desc: Animal psychology expert Watanabe Shigeru trained pigeons to differentiate between Picasso and Monet, and Java sparrows to recognize Bach. His work shows us what life as a scientist is all about.

programme.title: Core Kyoto Mini
programme.desc: Gifts are exchanged in the traditional betrothal rite. Paper cords are twisted into intricate shapes and decorate gifts.

programme.title: Easy Japanese for Work
programme.desc: Tuan-san, from Vietnam, works at an IT company. He wants to thank his superiors in Japanese. By role-playing and receiving guidance from experts, he'll learn a way to say thanks from the heart.

programme.title: Zero Waste Life
programme.desc: Artisan Takezawa Mutsumi's workshop lies at the foot of a mountain west of Tokyo. She makes pieces using leather from animals culled as pests, hides usually thrown away, but she gives them new life.

programme.title: Impacts: Climates Change the World
programme.desc: Average rainfall levels worldwide increased by 53 mm in 2024, with heavy rains in countries like Thailand causing major flooding and water damage among the worst in Asia.

programme.title: Where We Call Home
programme.desc: We meet Ashraf Baburi in Chiba Prefecture who provides work to women in his native Afghanistan by importing traditional rugs they weave, and French-born Stephane Danton who runs a tea store in Tokyo.

programme.title: HAGI: With the Flow
programme.desc: Locals in Hagi, on a delta facing the Sea of Japan, have lived in harmony with its waters. Beautiful time-lapse footage captures people crossing rivers by boat and canals flowing into homes.

programme.title: NHK Newsline
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Time-Lapse Japan
programme.desc: The Jomon period dates roughly from 13,000 to 300 BC. We examine various aspects of Jomon society, with footage from time-lapse creator Shimizu Daisuke. This time: Dogu.

programme.title: BOSAI
programme.desc: Heavy snow falling in a short time can threaten lives when it piles up on roofs, paralyzes roads, and brings social functions to a halt. We'll examine the reasons why heavy snows falls on Japan.

programme.title: Magical Japanese
programme.desc: The Japanese language is rich in words and expressions influenced by nature, history, and culture. This episode looks at words related to kaku, or "to write." Explore the unique culture behind them.

programme.title: Easy Japanese
programme.desc: #8 This is my friend, Ayaka-san. / To introduce someone to someone else, say a word to explain the relationship, followed by "no" and then the person's name and "desu".

programme.title: Samurai Sword Enthusiasts
programme.desc: The fact that foreign collectors cherish Japanese swords helps to unearth ancient works taken overseas after 1945. It also serves as a force for preserving traditional techniques facing extinction.

programme.title: Hello! NHK World Japan
programme.desc: We introduce a lineup of programs conveying Japan's unique perspectives and cultural practices. Themes include falconry, etiquette, and the National Museum of Japanese History.

programme.title: The World Heritage Sites in Japan
programme.desc: Shirakawa-go is one of Japan's snowiest regions, famous for its traditional thatched-roof Gassho-style homes. Every detail of life here is reflected in the cooperative bond shared by residents.

programme.title: Preparation
programme.desc: Luna, a girl who is uncomfortable with doctor's office, comes to understand the value of medicine via the thrilling explanations of a mysterious doctor. This episode is about doctor's office.

programme.title: Seasoning the Seasons

programme.desc: Al announces that he's given up his search for Rosa Coletti, a woman with whom he was involved with 20 years ago. However, he receives a letter from his mother saying that she's found Rosa.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie's motorcycle has seen better days and needs replacing. Joanie suggests to Chachi that they all pool their money with Fonzie's to surprise him with a new bike for his birthday.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.sub-title: It Only Hurts When I Smile
programme.desc: Fonzie comes down with a terrible toothache but refuses to see a dentist. Meanwhile, Howard is upset about Joanie's choice of attire for a wedding she's attending.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: After a day of everyone asking favours of him, Fonzie has a nightmare about a mad scientist draining his cool.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Chachi becomes jealous when he witnesses a passionate love scene between Joanie and an actor during rehearsals for a school play.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: After Jenny's date dumps her before a big masquerade ball, Fonzie agrees to take her. They dress as a bride and groom and, as part of a talent portion of the party, have a make-believe wedding.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Because she's never been to his house or met his mother, Joanie thinks that Chachi is ashamed of her. Fonzie invites the Cunningham's over to the Arcola's for dinner on Chachi's behalf.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie is shot, presumably by Potsie, during a weekend trip to Potsie's uncle's cabin. Fonzie, Chachi, and Roger each describe their versions of what happened.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Potsie falls behind on his rent and is forced to move back in with his parents. However, after learning that Lori Beth's roommate likes men who live alone, Potsie has to stay in his apartment.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Chachi discovers a great basketball player who recently transferred to Jefferson High.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Desperate to get Chachi on the school's basketball team, a scout for Eastern Florida University offers him a scholarship and several other superfluous perks, which seem too good to be true.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Richie and Lori Beth want to get married, but Lori Beth cannot go to Richie's base until they're already married. Roger suggests that they have a ceremony in which a proxy stands in for Richie.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Marion hurts her back before a bowling game, but Howard finds a new partner in the beautiful Fern Flagg. He had told Marion that she was an older woman, but when they meet, Marion is upset at his lying.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie takes his auto shop class to an out-of-town junkyard and meets a waitress at a diner whom he thinks is his mother.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Chachi struggles with an essay on the causes, hardships, and influences of immigration, until Fonzie tells him several stories in a series of musical vignettes.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Feeling Joanie and Chachi date too much, Howard offers to install a phone in her room if she agrees not to see Chachi for two weeks. She and Chachi try to find a way to get around this.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie is given the job as teacher of auto mechanics at Jefferson High School. He gets frustrated when his students don't pick everything up as quickly as he did when he was a kid.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Lori Beth receives a letter from Richie in which he proposes marriage. She has to go to Greenland for the wedding, but doesn't have the money to make the trip.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Roger Phillips, Marion's nephew, comes to Milwaukee to become an English teacher and the basketball coach at Jefferson High School. He gets off on the wrong foot with Fonzie.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Despite Howard's objection, Joanie decides to buy a car because she and Chachi are tired of having to double-date, and Chachi cannot afford to buy his own.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: On Christmas Eve, a terrible blizzard traps Howard and Joanie at the hardware store and Fonzie, Potsie, Chachi, Jenny, and Roger at Arnold's.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie wants to win the Teacher of the Year award to spite a fellow teacher.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.sub-title: It Only Hurts When I Smile
programme.desc: Fonzie comes down with a terrible toothache but refuses to see a dentist. Meanwhile, Howard is upset about Joanie's choice of attire for a wedding she's attending.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Feeling Joanie and Chachi date too much, Howard offers to install a phone in her room if she agrees not to see Chachi for two weeks. She and Chachi try to find a way to get around this.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie is given the job as teacher of auto mechanics at Jefferson High School. He gets frustrated when his students don't pick everything up as quickly as he did when he was a kid.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Lori Beth receives a letter from Richie in which he proposes marriage. She has to go to Greenland for the wedding, but doesn't have the money to make the trip.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Roger Phillips, Marion's nephew, comes to Milwaukee to become an English teacher and the basketball coach at Jefferson High School. He gets off on the wrong foot with Fonzie.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Despite Howard's objection, Joanie decides to buy a car because she and Chachi are tired of having to double-date, and Chachi cannot afford to buy his own.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: After a day of everyone asking favours of him, Fonzie has a nightmare about a mad scientist draining his cool.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Chachi becomes jealous when he witnesses a passionate love scene between Joanie and an actor during rehearsals for a school play.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: After Jenny's date dumps her before a big masquerade ball, Fonzie agrees to take her. They dress as a bride and groom and, as part of a talent portion of the party, have a make-believe wedding.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Because she's never been to his house or met his mother, Joanie thinks that Chachi is ashamed of her. Fonzie invites the Cunningham's over to the Arcola's for dinner on Chachi's behalf.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie is shot, presumably by Potsie, during a weekend trip to Potsie's uncle's cabin. Fonzie, Chachi, and Roger each describe their versions of what happened.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Potsie falls behind on his rent and is forced to move back in with his parents. However, after learning that Lori Beth's roommate likes men who live alone, Potsie has to stay in his apartment.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Chachi discovers a great basketball player who recently transferred to Jefferson High.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Desperate to get Chachi on the school's basketball team, a scout for Eastern Florida University offers him a scholarship and several other superfluous perks, which seem too good to be true.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Richie and Lori Beth want to get married, but Lori Beth cannot go to Richie's base until they're already married. Roger suggests that they have a ceremony in which a proxy stands in for Richie.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Marion hurts her back before a bowling game, but Howard finds a new partner in the beautiful Fern Flagg. He had told Marion that she was an older woman, but when they meet, Marion is upset at his lying.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie takes his auto shop class to an out-of-town junkyard and meets a waitress at a diner whom he thinks is his mother.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Chachi struggles with an essay on the causes, hardships, and influences of immigration, until Fonzie tells him several stories in a series of musical vignettes.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Joanie prepares some home movies to send to Richie. She mentions that she and Chachi have had their problems over the summer. In the movies, Lori Beth announces that she's going to have a baby.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie's Falcons and their rival gang, the War Mongers, attempt to settle their old scores with a game of softball. The outcome is disputed, so they end up rumbling.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.sub-title: Not With My Mother, You Don't
programme.desc: Al tells Chachi about his dull, mundane life, so Chachi suggests that he ask his mother out. However, they discover his mother is already seeing a mystery man.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Chachi tells Joanie that he wants to date other girls and soon realises that he's made a big mistake.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie happily agrees to be Lori Beth's natural childbirth coach in husband Richie's absence, until he finds out that he'll also have to go into the delivery room with her.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie and Roger are dating the same girl but don't know it. Meanwhile, Joanie is upset that she and Chachi don't go out as much as they used to.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie substitutes for Roger in his hygiene class, and, as a prank, the students hide the lesson plan and tell Fonzie they're in a sex education class.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: In order for Jenny to join the Rondells club, she has to steal a statue from Pfister Park and bring it to their clubhouse. Joanie is there to help, and they are both caught by Officer Kirk.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie is rejected several times by a new teacher at Jefferson High. After he kisses her in a final attempt to win her over, she screams and runs out of the house.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Joanie takes care of Richie Jr for the weekend, while Lori Beth visits Richie in Greenland. Howard and Marion go to New York on business, and Fonzie is away at a teachers' convention.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Dateless for an upcoming concert, Jenny is set up with Eugene Belvin by Joanie and Chachi. They, along with Fonzie, try to make Eugene appeal to Jenny by making him seem manly.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.sub-title: To Beanie Or Not To Beanie
programme.desc: Joanie has to decide where she wants to go to college, UCLA or the local UWM. She wants to go to UCLA, but Howard very much wants her to go to UWM.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: After seeing reports on television, Al feels compelled to visit the South to take part in an upcoming freedom march. Fonzie, concerned for his friend's wellbeing, decides to go with him.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie and Chachi's grandmother becomes very upset at them for suggesting that she move into a retirement home after they observe some signs of senility.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Al is chosen to head the Leopard Lodge's annual Poobah Doo Dah when he says he can get his distant cousin, Frankie Avalon, to perform.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: When Fonzie discovers that famous composer Tchaikovsky was very much like himself, he decides to get the entire Jefferson High gang to like classical music.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: The gang tries to get the Lone Ranger, Fonzie's idol, to appear at his birthday party. Meanwhile, Fonzie thinks about becoming a one-woman man.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie shows Roger a better way to attract women, Joanie and Chachi attempt songwriting, Potsie wants to join the Leopard Lodge, and Marion is up for the lead in a local musical production.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Roger entrusts his brother, Flip, to pick up Marion after he attends a concert, but he skips the concert to hang out with some hoodlums and leaves Marion to walk home in the cold.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: After a day of fishing with Howard, Chachi gets to thinking about his future when he realises that being a success in the music business is a long shot.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Jealous of Joanie's friendship with a new girl in school, Mikki, Jenny spreads a false rumour that Mikki is pregnant. As the rumour circulates, Fonzie is added to it as being the father.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: After a month of dating, Al wants to ask Chachi's mum to marry him. Being an old fashioned guy, Al asks Chachi's permission. He grants it, but doesn't think she'll want to marry Al.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Joanie prepares some home movies to send to Richie. She mentions that she and Chachi have had their problems over the summer. In the movies, Lori Beth announces that she's going to have a baby.

programme.desc: A new customer challenges Bill with his fully customized '37 Ford Coupe, which he wants brought back to the factory look.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Joanie prepares some home movies to send to Richie. She mentions that she and Chachi have had their problems over the summer. In the movies, Lori Beth announces that she's going to have a baby.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie's Falcons and their rival gang, the War Mongers, attempt to settle their old scores with a game of softball. The outcome is disputed, so they end up rumbling.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.sub-title: Not With My Mother, You Don't
programme.desc: Al tells Chachi about his dull, mundane life, so Chachi suggests that he ask his mother out. However, they discover his mother is already seeing a mystery man.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Chachi tells Joanie that he wants to date other girls and soon realises that he's made a big mistake.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie happily agrees to be Lori Beth's natural childbirth coach in husband Richie's absence, until he finds out that he'll also have to go into the delivery room with her.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie and Roger are dating the same girl but don't know it. Meanwhile, Joanie is upset that she and Chachi don't go out as much as they used to.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie substitutes for Roger in his hygiene class, and, as a prank, the students hide the lesson plan and tell Fonzie they're in a sex education class.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: In order for Jenny to join the Rondells club, she has to steal a statue from Pfister Park and bring it to their clubhouse. Joanie is there to help, and they are both caught by Officer Kirk.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Dateless for an upcoming concert, Jenny is set up with Eugene Belvin by Joanie and Chachi. They, along with Fonzie, try to make Eugene appeal to Jenny by making him seem manly.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.sub-title: To Beanie Or Not To Beanie
programme.desc: Joanie has to decide where she wants to go to college, UCLA or the local UWM. She wants to go to UCLA, but Howard very much wants her to go to UWM.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: After seeing reports on television, Al feels compelled to visit the South to take part in an upcoming freedom march. Fonzie, concerned for his friend's wellbeing, decides to go with him.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie and Chachi's grandmother becomes very upset at them for suggesting that she move into a retirement home after they observe some signs of senility.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Al is chosen to head the Leopard Lodge's annual Poobah Doo Dah when he says he can get his distant cousin, Frankie Avalon, to perform.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: When Fonzie discovers that famous composer Tchaikovsky was very much like himself, he decides to get the entire Jefferson High gang to like classical music.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: The gang tries to get the Lone Ranger, Fonzie's idol, to appear at his birthday party. Meanwhile, Fonzie thinks about becoming a one-woman man.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie shows Roger a better way to attract women, Joanie and Chachi attempt songwriting, Potsie wants to join the Leopard Lodge, and Marion is up for the lead in a local musical production.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Roger entrusts his brother, Flip, to pick up Marion after he attends a concert, but he skips the concert to hang out with some hoodlums and leaves Marion to walk home in the cold.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: After a day of fishing with Howard, Chachi gets to thinking about his future when he realises that being a success in the music business is a long shot.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Jealous of Joanie's friendship with a new girl in school, Mikki, Jenny spreads a false rumour that Mikki is pregnant. As the rumour circulates, Fonzie is added to it as being the father.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: After a month of dating, Al wants to ask Chachi's mum to marry him. Being an old fashioned guy, Al asks Chachi's permission. He grants it, but doesn't think she'll want to marry Al.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.sub-title: A Woman Not Under The Influence
programme.desc: It's love at first sight for Fonzie when he sees a woman as they pass each other on the escalator. Potsie knows who she is, and Fonzie vows to find her and make her his.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Joanie and Chachi come back from spending the summer in Chicago. Joanie wants to move there to be with Chachi and to continue their music career, however, Howard refuses to let her go.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Now that Joanie, Lori Beth, and Richie Jr have left the Cunningham house, Marion looks to find ways to cope and occupy her time.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: KC, Howard's niece, moves in with Howard and Marion while her parents look for a new boarding school. Marion asks Howard to consider letting her stay permanently.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie receives a good citizenship award from the Milwaukee Chamber of Commerce, but the ceremony is interrupted by a disgruntled childhood classmate of the Fonz who's been waiting 17 years for revenge.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Joanie and Chachi have the opportunity to appear on a television music show, but learn that their format is folk. Meanwhile, Fonzie is upset that folk music seems to be replacing rock and roll.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie pledges his love to Ashley, but risks losing her when he allows an old girlfriend to spend the night at his apartment. Meanwhile, Howard teaches KC to drive.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Marion encourages KC to date, so she accepts an invitation to Arnold's gala costume ball from Melvin Belvin.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.sub-title: There's No Business Like No Business
programme.desc: When a new hardware store in the local mall takes away all his business, Howard must decide whether to fight or give in to the competition and say goodbye to Cunningham Hardware.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie decides to help when Heather asks Santa to effect a reconciliation between her mother and her grandparents.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Roger's old flame from his school days shows up, and Roger cannot resist her. Fonzie attempts to prevent her from breaking his heart again.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Ashley receives an invitation to her estranged parents' wedding anniversary party. She doesn't want to go, but Fonzie insists.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Flip goes out with some friends who drink and become intoxicated. While under the influence, the driver hits Heather with his car, severely injuring her.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie hires a convict, via a work-release program, to be a mechanic at his garage.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.sub-title: Life Is More Important Than Show Business
programme.desc: A record producer hears a demonstration tape of Joanie and Chachi and is eager to sign them.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie and Ashley remember the night of their momentous first date together when the Cunninghams throw them a surprise six-month anniversary party.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: It's Heather's birthday, but Fonzie has to report for the Army reserves. He promises Heather that he will be at her party, but he didn't count on Officer Kirk being the commanding officer.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonz and Roger are going to a teacher's convention in another city, but miss their flight. They hire Potsie's uncle to fly them in his old crop dusting plane, which could lead to high flying disaster.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie is the best man at the wedding of an old pal, but the bride-to-be stops the ceremony after spotting Fonzie. Meanwhile, KC awaits word about a part in the school musical.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Fonzie babysits Heather when Ashley leaves for a tax seminar, but his attention is drawn away by a televised heavyweight title fight, leaving Heather resentful toward him.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: After deciding to quit the band, Joanie moves back in with Howard and Marion to enrol in a teaching program at the University of Wisconsin. Chachi tries to convince her to stay with him.

programme.title: Happy Days
programme.desc: Jenny dumps a nice guy to be with a former Olympic swimmer. Meanwhile, Fonzie wants to have a swimming pool put in the Cunninghams' backyard.

programme.title: Direct Talk

programme.desc: Sekiguchi Itaru helped to restore the pipe organ at Notre Dame in Paris, following the cathedral's fire in 2019. He tells us about his work, including the tuning and voicing of these instruments.

programme.sub-title: Please Hold While We Connect You

programme.sub-title: Remote Out of Control / Critter Comfort

programme.title: Fifi and the Flowertots
programme.desc: Violet is organising a flower festival. She is so busy helping the other tots with their blooms she has neglected hers. Will her flower open in time for the big day?

programme.title: Fifi and the Flowertots

programme.title: Madagascar: A Little Wild
programme.desc: When Melman loses his first tooth, he fears he won't be Kate's baby anymore and goes on a city-wide chase to recover his tooth before Kate can replace him with a younger, cuter animal.

programme.desc: When Gabby unboxes a recipe for dandelion soup and a soup bowl, she discovers Kitty Fairy is sick! Gabby and Pandy make her some delicious dandelion soup and while she rests, they take care of her Garden.

programme.title: Gabby's Dollhouse
programme.desc: When Gabby unboxes a new camera that takes pictures with silly filters, Cat and Rat use it to lead Gabby, Pandy, and the Gabby Cats on a photographic scavenger hunt around the dollhouse.

programme.title: Madagascar: A Little Wild
